tex:
(a) Here is a list of ingredients to make 20 biscuits.

\begin{tabular}{l}
260 g of butter \\
500 g of sugar \\
650 g of flour \\
425 g of rice
\end{tabular}

(i) (b) Find the mass of rice as a percentage of the mass of sugar.

(c) Find the mass of butter needed to make 35 of these biscuits.

(d) Michel has 2 kg of each ingredient.

Work out the greatest number of these biscuits that he can make.

(e) A company makes these biscuits at a cost of \$1.35 per packet.
These biscuits are sold for \$1.89 per packet.

(i) (f) Calculate the percentage profit the company makes on each packet.

(g) The selling price of \$1.89 has increased by 8\% from last year.

Calculate the selling price last year.

\$ 

(h) Over a period of 3 years, the company's sales of biscuits increased from 15.6 million packets to 20.8 million packets.
The sales increased exponentially by the same percentage each year.

Calculate the percentage increase each year.

(i) The people who work for the company are in the following age groups.

\begin{tabular}{lll}
Group A & Group B & Group C \\
Under 30 years & 30 to 50 years & Over 50 years
\end{tabular}

The ratio of the number in group A to the number in group B is 7 : 10.
The ratio of the number in group B to the number in group C is 4 : 3.

(i) (j) Find the ratio of the number in group A to the number in group C.
Give your answer in its simplest form.

 : 

(k) There are 45 people in group C.

Find the total number of people who work for the company.

500 g of sugar

The sales increased exponentially by the same percentage each year. Calculate the percentage increase each year. (i) The people who work for the company are in the following age groups.
Group AGroup BGroup C
Under 30 years30 to 50 yearsOver 50 years
The ratio of the number in group A to the number in group B is 7 : 10. The ratio of the number in group B to the number in group C is 4 : 3. (i) (j) Find the ratio of the number in group A to the number in group C. Give your answer in its simplest form. : (k) There are 45 people in group C. Find the total number of people who work for the company.

\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & $20 \leq t < 25$ & $25 \leq t < 30$ & $30 \leq t < 35$ & $35 \leq t < 40$ & $40 \leq t < 45$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 44 & 32 & 28 & 12 & 4 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(a) (i) (b) Write down the modal time interval.

(e) A student is chosen at random.

Find the probability that this student takes more than 40 minutes.

(f) (i) (g) Complete the cumulative frequency table.

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Time ($t$ minutes) & $t < 20$ & $t < 25$ & $t < 30$ & $t < 35$ & $t < 40$ & $t < 45$ \\
\hline
Cumulative frequency & 0 & 44 &  &  &  &  \\
\hline
\end{tabular}

(h) On the grid, draw a cumulative frequency diagram to show this information.

(i) Find the median time.

(j) Find the interquartile range.

(k) Find the number of students who took more than 37 minutes to complete the cooking challenge.
